name: Durability
description: Items track condition and lose durability through use, damage, or environment until they require repair or break.
design_notes: Durability is item-instance metadata, not a property that can live only on an item definition. Designers should decide which durability_loss_rule applies to each action, what the broken state prevents, how repair recipes work, and how UI preview helps players avoid losing critical tools unexpectedly.
design_purpose: Create long-term resource pressure through maintenance, repair, replacement, and tool choice.
Prevent one early item from solving every future survival or combat problem.
Connect crafting materials, gathering tools, economy sinks, and exploration readiness.
player_fantasy: The player feels their gear is valuable and must be maintained to survive longer journeys.

parameters.name: durability_item_id
parameters.description: Item instance identifier that owns current condition and repair metadata.

parameters.name: durability_points_max
parameters.description: Maximum condition points stored on each instance before wear modifiers.

parameters.name: durability_loss_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that maps actions, impacts, or exposure events into condition loss.

parameters.name: repair_recipe_id
parameters.description: Recipe used to restore condition, often with station, material, or skill requirements.

parameters.name: broken_state_id
parameters.description: State applied when current condition crosses the broken threshold.

parameters.name: condition_warning_threshold_id
parameters.description: UI warning table used to notify players before important gear fails.

edge_cases: An item breaks during the same action that gathers resources, deals damage, or blocks an attack.
Repair materials are consumed but the item instance is moved, traded, or destroyed during the transaction.
Stacked items share one icon but contain different durability_item_id values and condition states.
Server correction changes durability after the client already displayed the item as usable.
common_bugs: Durability is stored on the item definition, so all copies of the tool lose condition together.
Broken items still pass action validation because only UI checks condition thresholds.
Repair preview and repair transaction use different material costs or restored condition rules.
Condition loss is applied on both client prediction and server authority, doubling wear.
balancing_notes: Fast gear wear can add survival tension, but it becomes frustrating if repair materials are scarce or unclear.
Durability works best when players can choose between cheap disposable tools and costly reliable equipment.
Critical gear should warn before breaking unless sudden failure is a deliberate high-stakes design choice.
accessibility_notes: Show durability with numbers or labels in addition to bars or color.
Provide repair prompts or warning reminders before players enter long routes with nearly broken key gear.

implementation_notes.unity: Store condition on item_instance_id records and route all wear through durability_loss_rule_id events. Repair should reserve the item and materials, validate repair_recipe_id, mutate condition, and commit through one inventory transaction.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep durability metadata in inventory Resources or dictionaries per instance. Tool use should ask the durability service to apply loss before or after the action according to rule data, then emit UI updates from threshold crossings.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use replicated item instance structs or inventory components to own condition. The server should validate wear and repair, while clients can preview condition changes and receive authoritative correction for broken_state_id.
implementation_notes.web: Represent each durable item as an instance record with current condition, max points, and broken state. Keep repair and wear transactions deterministic so local saves, multiplayer, and replay do not diverge.

relationships.reason: Durability loss creates repair decisions that consume currency, materials, or service access.
Item durability needs inventory metadata, warning states, and repair previews attached to item instances.
Rare equipment durability must avoid punishing players into ignoring high-value drops.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses tool and armor durability as part of crafting and resource planning.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses weapon durability to encourage varied equipment use.
